title: REC-1: Add activation.tier field to agents-meta schema and tier-based rule loading
description: Add optional activation.tier field (always/path/description) to AgentsMetaNode interface and agentsMetaNodeBaseSchema. Modify loadRulesForPath() in get-rules.ts to branch on tier before minimatch: always→include directly, description→return stub (path + description, no content), path→existing minimatch logic. Update RulesPayload and RulesEntry types to support description stubs. Default tier='path' ensures zero breaking changes.

files.change: Add optional activation?: { tier: 'always' | 'path' | 'description'; description?: string } field to AgentsMetaNode interface

Add activation: z.object({ tier: z.enum(['always','path','description']), description: z.string().optional() }).optional() to schema; update withDerivedAgentsMetaNodeDefaults to preserve activation field

files.target: loadRulesForPath() L136-161 and RulesPayload type L22-27
files.change: Add DescriptionStub type (path + description, no content). Extend RulesEntry union or add separate stub array. In loadRulesForPath(), add tier branch before minimatch: always→include without glob check, description→push stub (read description from node, skip file read), path→existing minimatch. Update RulesPayload to include description_stubs?: DescriptionStub[]

implementation: Add activation type to AgentsMetaNode interface in agents.ts with optional activation field
Add activation zod schema to agentsMetaNodeBaseSchema in agents-meta.ts, ensure optional with no default (undefined means path behavior)
Define DescriptionStub type in get-rules.ts: { path: string; description: string }
Extend RulesPayload with description_stubs?: DescriptionStub[] field
Rewrite loadRulesForPath() to pre-filter by tier: always nodes skip minimatch, description nodes produce stubs, path nodes use existing minimatch logic
Verify backward compatibility: run existing tests, ensure nodes without activation field still behave as path tier

reference.pattern: Optional zod field with no default (undefined = legacy behavior)
reference.examples: Follow existing zod optional pattern; match existing agentsMetaNodeBaseSchema field style

criteria: AgentsMetaNode interface has activation?: { tier: 'always' | 'path' | 'description'; description?: string }
agentsMetaNodeBaseSchema parses nodes with and without activation field without error
loadRulesForPath() returns node content directly for tier=always (no glob check)
loadRulesForPath() returns DescriptionStub (no file content read) for tier=description
loadRulesForPath() preserves existing minimatch behavior for tier=path and undefined activation
RulesPayload.description_stubs field populated correctly when description-tier nodes match
Existing test suite passes with zero modifications to test files

rationale.chosen_approach: Optional field with undefined-as-path-default avoids any schema migration or breaking change
decision_factors: Aligns with Windsurf/Cursor/Claude Code industry standard
rationale.tradeoffs: description_stubs is a new array in RulesPayload — clients must handle it gracefully (undefined-safe access)

manual_checks: Create a test agents.meta.json node with activation.tier='always' and verify it loads without glob match
Create a node with tier='description' and verify stub returned (no file read, description text present)
Verify existing node without activation field still loads via minimatch
success_metrics: All 3 tier branches execute in loadRulesForPath without regression
TypeScript compilation passes with zero new type errors

risks.description: Clients reading RulesPayload may not handle new description_stubs field
risks.mitigation: Make description_stubs optional in RulesPayload type (?: not :)
risks.fallback: Default to empty array [] if field absent

interfaces.name: DescriptionStub
interfaces.purpose: Lightweight rule stub returned for description-tier nodes, no content loaded

functions.signature: loadRulesForPath(projectRoot, meta, path): Promise<{ rules: LoadedRule[]; stubs: DescriptionStub[] }>
functions.purpose: Returns full rule content for always/path tiers, description stubs for description tier
